notes_for_human_reviewer: INTERMEDIATE-HIGH — a thoughtful, well-judged answer that passes comfortably. Engages the honest questions from real evidence ('the judgment moments... are the parts I find most interesting, not the parts I dread'), names the role change (own vs support), flags the genuine uncertainty (public accountability), and proposes concrete stretch asks (draft the sponsor pre-read, lead a small workshop) framed as a development conversation, not a title demand. It sits at 4s rather than a clean 5 because it's a notch lighter than hire-ready on a couple of fronts — it names two stretch asks but doesn't connect them to the full set of new skills (sponsor management AND strategic framing AND accountability) or mention observing/mentoring, and the realism about costs is present but brief. Expect 4s across; a solid, genuinely good answer.
